title: Namespace selection and query scoping
purpose: Make the dashboard multi-tenant-aware: a selected namespace scopes every query and every subscription so an operator investigates one tenant cleanly.
task: Build the namespace feature: a context holding the selected namespace (loaded from listNamespaces), a selector to switch it, and the scoping wiring so every query key and every subscription filter carries the selected namespace and re-scopes on switch.

requirements.title: Namespace context and selector
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a namespace context holding the selected namespace (populated from listNamespaces) and a selector component letting the operator switch namespaces.
acceptance: src/features/namespace/context/NamespaceContext.tsx exposes the selected namespace and a setter, with the available namespaces loaded via the api client's listNamespaces.
NamespaceSelector renders the available namespaces (shadcn select) and updates the context on change.
The selector renders loading and error states for the namespace list.
A test asserts selecting a namespace updates the context value.

requirements.title: Scoping every query and subscription
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL scope every query and every subscription by the selected namespace, and WHEN the namespace changes THE SYSTEM SHALL re-scope both the active query and the live subscription. No view SHALL issue an unscoped query when a namespace is selected.
acceptance: The list query key, the history query key, and the subscription filter all include the selected namespace.
Changing the namespace invalidates/re-issues the active query and re-establishes the active subscription under the new namespace.
A test switching namespace asserts the query is re-issued and the subscription re-established with the new namespace, and that no query is issued without a namespace when one is selected.

boundaries: SHALL NOT define the REST client or WebSocket manager — AU-002, AU-003 (this brief threads the namespace through them).
SHALL NOT build the list or detail views — AU-004, AU-005 (this brief provides the namespace they scope by).
SHALL NOT define server namespace semantics — that is cluster AW.
verification: bun test passes for the namespace context and scoping tests.
No unscoped query when a namespace is selected; no lint-disable comments added.
